description: Set contact detection tolerances. Controls the size of extent used when inserting balls into cell space for contact detection. The extent size includes: minimum extent + 2 × (max interaction distance + constant tolerance + extent tolerance × max extent length + velocity tolerance × max velocity component). Ball is remapped when cumulative translation exceeds this sum. See cycle operations 30.0 (Update Spatial Searching) and 35.0 (Create/delete Contacts) for details.

python_sdk_alternative.workaround: itasca.command('ball tolerance ...') - Python SDK does not provide tolerance settings

keywords.name: constant
keywords.description: Assign the constant contact-detection tolerance. This is assigned on a per-ball basis.

keywords.name: extent
keywords.description: Assign the extent contact-detection tolerance. This is a global setting for all balls.

keywords.name: velocity
keywords.description: Assign the velocity contact-detection tolerance. This is a global setting for all balls.

examples.description: Set constant tolerance to 0.01 for all balls
Set constant tolerance for specific ball group
Set global velocity tolerance for fast-moving simulations
Clean model before changing extent tolerance (required after restore)
